question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: LDAP-S / AD secure-bind failures tied to domain controller certificates after PKI/template changes: a prior ticket where DC certificates were re-enrolled from the internal CA after an auto-enrollment template fix, restoring LDAP‑S functionality (TI-1700008)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 13:
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF80: LDAP-S queries to DCs failing for some applications
Ticket number: TI-1700008
Company: Customer Company 80 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 31
Owner: Employee 32
Source: Email
Type: Support Request
Created on: 13.02.25 10:20

Closure
Closure date: 21.02.25 13:55
Closure text: Domain Controller certificates re-enrolled from internal CA after auto-enrollment template fix; LDAP-S queries restored.

Activities
Emails:

13.02.25 10:20 KF80: Internal applications cannot bind to AD over LDAP-S Contact Person 31 Helpdesk
Hello,
Our identity team reported that several internal applications which use LDAP-S (LDAP over TLS port 636) to authenticate against Active Directory have been failing since this morning. Examples include our HR system's user provisioning, the on-prem SharePoint for user lookup, and the network access control (NAC) system.
The affected applications report "could not establish secure connection to LDAP server" or "TLS handshake failed against domain controller". Plain LDAP (port 389) still works but our security policy requires LDAP-S.
We suspect this is related to the PKI work last weekend.
Thanks
Contact Person 31

13.02.25 10:25 Ticket created: TI-1700008. Priority High - identity infrastructure impact.

13.02.25 10:35 Ticket assigned | TI-1700008
Assigned to Employee 32 (BU Infrastructure - PKI/Network).

13.02.25 14:00 Re: KF80: LDAP-S queries failing Employee 32 Contact Person 31
Hello,
Thanks. Could you please confirm:
- Whether ALL DCs are affected or only some
- The output from a DC of: certutil -store MY (look for the "Domain Controller" or "Kerberos Authentication" certificates and check expiry/issuer)
- When the symptom started exactly (yesterday, today morning?)

13.02.25 17:50 Re: KF80: LDAP-S queries failing Contact Person 31 Employee 32
Hi,
- All 4 DCs in the main site are affected; the secondary site DCs were not yet checked
- Sample DC: cert in MY store shows the DC cert ISSUED 14.02.2024 (so almost a year ago) by the OLD issuing CA. It hasn't expired yet but applications complain about it
- Started overnight - applications worked yesterday afternoon, failed by morning

21.02.25 13:55 Ticket closed | TI-1700008
Closure description:
Root cause: The Domain Controllers were configured to auto-enroll for the "Domain Controller Authentication" certificate template via Group Policy. Last year's certificate had not yet expired but auto-enrollment normally triggers renewal at the 60%
is_rec_reason: This is a factual report of a prior ticket describing actions taken (re-enrollment after a template fix) that restored functionality, not a recommendation or instruction.
groundedness_reason: TI-1700008 documents LDAP-S failures and the closure states Domain Controller certificates were re-enrolled from the internal CA after an auto-enrollment template fix, which restored LDAP-S queries, matching the ANSWER.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Internal HTTPS services showing browser trust warnings after a PKI change: a past case where an issuing CA was renewed over a weekend produced NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID across internal sites; the resolution required publishing the intermediate CA to AIA/CRL and validation by engineers (TI-1700005)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 55:
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF80: Internal portal showing TLS certificate errors after PKI renewal
Ticket number: TI-1700005
Company: Customer Company 80 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 31
Owner: Employee 32
Source: Email
Type: Support Request
Created on: 11.02.25 08:30

Closure
Closure date: 19.02.25 11:48
Closure text: Intermediate CA certificate published correctly to AIA/CRL distribution points; affected services validated.

Activities
Emails:

11.02.25 08:30 KF80: Internal application throwing certificate errors Contact Person 31 Helpdesk
Hello,
Since this morning, our internal HR portal (https://hrportal.kf80.local) is showing certificate warnings in the browser when accessed from any client. The error in Chrome is "NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID" and in Edge it says the certificate is not trusted.
The portal was working fine yesterday. The only change I am aware of is that our PKI team renewed the internal Certificate Authority certificates over the weekend, which we were told would be transparent to users.
This is blocking access to HR records and the absence reporting system.
Thanks
Contact Person 31

11.02.25 08:35 Ticket created: TI-1700005
Confirmation.

11.02.25 08:40 Ticket priority High - operational impact on internal services.

11.02.25 08:55 Ticket assigned | TI-1700005
Assigned to Employee 32 (BU Infrastructure - PKI/Network).

11.02.25 13:15 Re: KF80: Internal application certificate errors Employee 32 Contact Person 31
Hello Contact Person 31,
Thanks. To investigate the post-renewal cert chain, could you please:
- Confirm what was renewed on the weekend (the issuing CA, the root, both?)
- Send the output of running on a client: certutil -urlfetch -verify against the portal cert (or just attach the cert chain shown in browser → Inspect → Security)
- Confirm whether this affects only this portal or also other internal HTTPS services

11.02.25 16:50 Re: KF80: Internal application certificate errors Contact Person 31 Employee 32
Hi,
The PKI team confirmed they renewed the issuing CA certificate (KF80-Issuing-CA-01) on Saturday evening. The root CA was not changed.
Browser inspection shows: Certificate is signed by KF80-Issuing-CA-01, but the issuing CA cert in the chain shows "Issuer's certificate not found" and the chain breaks at the issuing CA.
We checked two other internal HTTPS services and they are also showing similar warnings (one of them is our internal SharePoint farm).

19.02.25 11:48 Ticket closed | TI-1700005
Closure description:
is_rec_reason: This statement recounts a past incident and describes how it was resolved, without framing a recommendation or advising future action.
groundedness_reason: The TI-1700005 ticket describes internal HTTPS services showing NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID after the issuing CA (KF80-Issuing-CA-01) was renewed over the weekend, and the closure states the intermediate CA was published to AIA/CRL distribution points and affected services were validated.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: VPN authentication failing for many remote users after the weekend PKI work: a prior incident where the new VPN server certificate was issued from a refreshed template that omitted a required EKU; resolution involved reissuing certificates from a corrected template and deploying them via GPO (TI-1700006)
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 104:
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF80: VPN authentication failing for users after weekend
Ticket number: TI-1700006
Company: Customer Company 80 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 31
Owner: Employee 33
Source: Email
Type: Support Request
Created on: 11.02.25 09:45

Closure
Closure date: 18.02.25 14:25
Closure text: VPN clients reissued certificates from a new template that includes the corrected EKU set; new certificates deployed via GPO; affected users validated.

Activities
Emails:

11.02.25 09:45 KF80: VPN connection refusing to authenticate Contact Person 31 Helpdesk
Hi,
This morning many users from home are reporting that the company VPN no longer connects. The client (we use IKEv2 with certificate-based auth) shows "authentication failed" or "could not validate server certificate" errors depending on the client OS.
About 80 home-working users are blocked. We use SCEP-issued user certificates from our internal PKI for VPN authentication.
Note: yesterday IT mentioned they renewed something in the PKI over the weekend.
Best regards
Contact Person 31

11.02.25 09:48 Ticket created: TI-1700006
Confirmation. Priority High due to scope.

11.02.25 09:55 Ticket assigned | TI-1700006
Assigned to Employee 33 (BU Infrastructure - Network/Remote Access).

11.02.25 12:30 Re: KF80: VPN authentication failing Employee 33 Contact Person 31
Hello,
Thanks. Helpful info to gather:
- VPN concentrator make/model and version
- Whether server-side cert was also renewed during the weekend PKI work
- Sample IKEv2 negotiation log from the VPN concentrator for a failing connection (if available)
- Whether any user certificates were re-issued during/after the PKI renewal

11.02.25 17:20 Re: KF80: VPN authentication failing Contact Person 31 Employee 33
Hi,
- VPN concentrator: CheckPoint cluster, latest stable
- Server-side cert: yes, renewed Saturday from the same internal CA
- Logs show "client cert chain validation failed" / "EKU mismatch"
- User certs: PKI team says no user certs were re-issued; they have not expired

18.02.25 14:25 Ticket closed | TI-1700006
Closure description:
Root cause: The new server-side VPN certificate was issued from a refreshed certificate template after the weekend PKI work. The new template inadvertently included only the "Server Authentication" Extended Key Usage (EKU) and dropped the "IP security IKE intermediate" EKU that the previous template had, which the CheckPoint VPN configuration required.
The user-side certificates were unchanged but the IKEv2 mutual authentication step now
is_rec_reason: The statement reports a past incident and its resolution (reissuing certificates and deploying via GPO) without offering recommendations, directives, or conditional advice.
groundedness_reason: The ticket TI-1700006 describes the VPN outage after weekend PKI work, states the new server cert was issued from a refreshed template that dropped the required IP security IKE EKU, and records reissuing client certificates from a corrected template and deploying them via GPO as the resolution.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: These three examples show the same pattern: a PKI/CA change caused multiple, cross‑service failures and each required engineering changes to CA/template/publishing and certificate re-issuance or re-enrollment — actions that were performed by infrastructure/PKI engineers in those tickets
